releases.title: Automatic generated-file housekeeping
releases.summary: PAC now cleans generated update/download/debug/build artifacts automatically on startup, after updates, and from Update Center.
changes: Added a generated-file housekeeping service that runs on startup and after release updates.
Added GET/POST /v1/updates/housekeeping for status, preview, and cleanup.
Extended cleanup to update downloads, extracted updates, rollback backups, release asset cache, debug bundles, stale binary cache, old logs, and app-tree generated build/cache folders.
Added Update Center housekeeping controls with Preview cleanup and Clean now actions.
Added storage/category summaries and reclaimed-byte reporting so large growth categories are visible.
Documented the generated artifact retention policy.

releases.title: Source zip artifact hygiene validation
releases.summary: Prevent generated release output from leaking into PAC source/update zips and add a reusable source zip validator.
changes: Added scripts/validate-source-zip.py to fail source/update artifacts containing dist/, release-binaries/, removed binary source folders, __pycache__, or compiled Python files.
Strengthened scripts/generate-pac-release.py validation so source/update zips reject dist/ and nested release-binaries output, not only root release-binaries/.
Updated GitHub release workflow validation to run the source zip validator after packaging.
Regenerated the full source zip without the stray dist/release-binaries/RELEASE_BINARIES.json entry found in 1.0.427.
not_done: This pass does not change runtime housekeeping behavior; it fixes packaging/audit issues found while checking the current zip.

releases.title: Generated download housekeeping
releases.summary: Make Downloads housekeeping effective for generated binary artifacts and temporary debug/support bundles.
changes: Changed binary artifact pruning so Keep newest only removes deprecated binary folders, stale versions, and duplicate unversioned platform builds instead of relying only on semantic version groups.
Added generated-file housekeeping helpers for debug bundles and update temporary/cache files.
Added a debug bundle cleanup API and a Clear old bundles action in the Downloads modal.
Environment and session debug bundles now skip old update-backup log tails so support downloads are focused on current runtime logs.
Added update temp housekeeping API for release asset cache, extracted packages, upload temp files, and old rollback backups.
Documented generated download retention policy in docs/generated-download-housekeeping-20260530.md.
not_done: The Update Center has an API for update-temp housekeeping, but the UI does not yet expose a dedicated update-cache cleanup card.
Retention values are fixed defaults in this pass; configurable housekeeping policy can be added later.

releases.title: Release channel sync for local development versions
releases.summary: Allow the Update Center to apply the official GitHub release channel even when the running local development build has the same or a higher semantic PAC version.
changes: Added release decision metadata so update checks expose version_comparison, update_reason, can_apply_update, and local_release_identity.
Changed update checks to treat local_version_ahead and same_version_newer_release_build as applyable release-channel sync states.
Updated the release apply API so it no longer blocks applyable release-channel syncs when semantic version ordering alone would say there is no newer update.
Updated the Update Center UI to show release-channel sync availability when local direct development has advanced the local version ahead of GitHub Releases.
Documented the release-channel sync model and added it to the technical design notes.

releases.title: Update orchestrator and binary refresh
releases.summary: Add an Update Center environment orchestration phase that resolves release binaries, refreshes PAC/pi.dev tool instructions, and validates macOS binary targets in the binary-first release workflow.
changes: Added a controller update orchestrator that downloads RELEASE_BINARIES.json, resolves host pac-endpoint and pacctl direct GitHub Release assets, verifies checksums, and installs them into the local PAC binary cache.
Added Update Center APIs and UI cards for previewing and running the environment refresh independently of the source update.
Changed release apply so the Update Center runs the environment refresh after applying the PAC source package.
Generated PAC/pi.dev tool instruction files under PACP_HOME/agent/tool-instructions so pi.dev guidance reflects the two-binary pac-endpoint/pacctl model.
Expanded release asset discovery to include direct pac-endpoint-* and pacctl-* assets, not only aggregate release zips and manifests.
Validated that the release binary workflow includes macOS/OSX darwin/amd64 and darwin/arm64 targets.
Fixed the pi.dev harness Dockerfile to build pacctl from the full multi-file source tree.

releases.title: Durable workspace command event store
releases.summary: Move workspace command streaming output into a durable append-only event store and add UI replay of stored terminal history.
changes: Added WorkspaceAgentCommandEvent and a workspace_agent_command_events table for append-only command output storage.
Updated workspace command streaming to replay from the durable event store by sequence cursor.
Added a command events replay API for terminal history and later UI/history tooling.
Changed command records to keep only a bounded recent event tail while retaining aggregate stdout/stderr summaries.
Extended the workspace live terminal with a command history rail that can replay stored events after a workspace container disconnects.
Updated documentation to mark the first durable command event store as implemented and move retention/search/export into follow-up work.

releases.title: Endpoint telemetry cards and workspace terminal interrupt
releases.summary: Add endpoint hardware/metrics cards, a UI live terminal for workspace agents, workspace command cancellation/interrupt handling, and document the remaining durable event-store follow-up.
changes: Added endpoint detail hardware and metrics cards using pac-endpoint inventory and metric heartbeat data.
Added a Workspaces live terminal action for online workspace agents that queues commands through PAC and streams newline-delimited command events into the UI.
Added workspace command cancel/interrupt API support and pacctl workspace cancel/interrupt commands.
Updated pacctl wait/stream timeout behavior to request a workspace command interrupt instead of leaving remote commands running silently.
Updated pac-endpoint workspace execution to poll PAC for cancel requests and terminate running workspace commands with interrupted status.
Documented the missing high-volume command event model so event replay/retention can be moved out of the command payload in a later pass.

releases.title: Workspace command live streaming
releases.summary: Add live PAC-routed stdout/stderr streaming for workspace commands while preserving centralized command history in PAC.
changes: Added ordered command event storage to workspace-agent command records so live output and history use the same PAC-owned source of truth.
Added workspace-agent command event ingestion and newline-delimited stream APIs for live stdout/stderr/status delivery.
Updated pac-endpoint workspace command execution to pipe stdout/stderr and post incremental events while commands run.
Added pacctl workspace exec --stream to print live command output and return the remote workspace command exit code.
Documented live workspace command streaming in the binary/workspace architecture, technical design doc, and binary READMEs.

releases.title: Endpoint telemetry and workspace agent status
releases.summary: Forward lightweight hardware inventory and runtime metrics from pac-endpoint host/workspace heartbeats, expose the data on workspace-agent APIs, and show online workspace agents in the Workspaces UI.
changes: Added pac-endpoint inventory and metrics collectors for host identity, OS/arch, kernel, CPU, memory, disk usage, network interfaces, tool readiness, load, and memory usage.
Forwarded inventory and metrics through host endpoint heartbeat capabilities/metadata so endpoints are observable without bundling an external metrics stack.
Forwarded refreshed inventory and metrics through pac-endpoint workspace run heartbeats so workspace containers report runtime health over the outbound PAC connection.
Extended workspace-agent register/heartbeat payloads and public workspace status responses with inventory and metrics fields stored in PAC metadata.
Updated the Workspaces active side panel to show registered workspace agents, online/degraded/offline state, root path, and latest load or memory usage hints.
Documented endpoint telemetry and workspace liveness behavior in the binary/workspace architecture and technical design docs.

releases.title: Binary-first main release workflow and workspace exec wait
releases.summary: Run the binary-first release pipeline automatically on main changes, mirror workflows into the GitHub-native workflow path, and let pacctl wait for routed workspace commands through PAC.
changes: Added .github/workflows/pac-release.yml and mirrored existing PAC workflows so GitHub can run the release pipeline directly from repository main.
Changed the release workflow to run on pushes to main while ignoring release metadata-only commits to avoid release loops.
Kept the binary compile step before release packaging and added scripts/validate-release-binary-pipeline.py to fail the workflow if that order or the two-binary asset contract drifts.
Added GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/commands/{command_id} so clients can poll command status and retrieve stored output/history from PAC.
Updated pacctl workspace exec with --wait and --timeout support so commands can be queued through PAC and waited on without direct container access.
Updated workspace-agent status presentation so stopped containers appear degraded/offline from heartbeat age instead of remaining indefinitely online.
Updated release and binary architecture documentation for automatic main release builds and source-only packages with direct GitHub binary assets.

releases.title: Workspace agent APIs and two-binary cleanup
releases.summary: Add controller-side workspace-agent registration/command APIs, retire the pac-agent and zed-binary source folders, and move MCP/editor behavior into pacctl while keeping release binaries limited to pac-endpoint and pacctl.
changes: Added persistent workspace agent and workspace command models plus SQLite storage for registered workspace agents and queued command history.
Added /v1/workspace-agents register, heartbeat, command claim, and command completion APIs used by pac-endpoint workspace run over outbound HTTPS.
Added /v1/workspaces list/status and /v1/workspaces/{id}/commands so pacctl can route commands through PAC to online workspace agents.
Added pacctl mcp serve as the replacement for the old zed-binary MCP helper.
Removed binaries/pac-agent and binaries/zed-binary from the source tree; pac-endpoint and pacctl are now the only Go binary products.
Updated the Source Library, MCP build route, UI build shortcut, release binary manifest, and documentation to use pacctl instead of zed-binary.
Added scripts/install-pac-binary.sh for direct GitHub Release asset installation of pac-endpoint or pacctl without bundling compiled binaries in the source zip.
Extended the binary/workspace design documentation with the implemented workspace-agent API flow and deleted-binary cleanup state.

releases.title: Two-binary endpoint/workspace architecture design pass
releases.summary: Document and begin implementing the two-binary PAC model: pac-endpoint as host/workspace wrapper and pacctl as the controller client, with direct GitHub release binary assets and no bundled binaries.
changes: Added the binary/workspace-agent architecture design file covering GitHub release assets, install/update behavior, workspace registration, and cleanup sequencing.
Removed the obsolete pac-endpoint-runner source directory; runner behavior is embedded in pac-endpoint.
Restricted release binary compilation to the two primary installable binaries: pac-endpoint and pacctl.
Changed release generation to copy direct binary assets such as pac-endpoint-linux-amd64 and pacctl-linux-amd64 into dist for GitHub Release upload.
Added pac-endpoint workspace run/register mode to read JSON/env config, register workspace agents over outbound HTTPS, heartbeat, and poll routed commands with endpoint API fallback.
Added pacctl API, polling, provider send, and workspace status/exec command entry points for controller-routed workflows.
Updated install.sh to stop building legacy Zed/MCP binaries during controller installation and document GitHub release asset resolution instead.
Updated binary READMEs and the technical design document with the new two-binary model.

releases.title: GitHub binary artifacts without bundled source zips
releases.summary: Keep compiled binaries as GitHub release assets, remove them from PAC source/update zips, and resolve/download them at runtime before falling back to a local build.
changes: Changed release generation so pac-full.zip and pac-patch.zip no longer include release-binaries/ compiled outputs.
Kept pac-binaries.zip and RELEASE_BINARIES.json as dedicated GitHub Release assets for compiled component downloads.
Added release zip validation in the GitHub workflow to fail when source/update zips accidentally bundle binaries.
Extended update release asset endpoints so the UI/runtime can list, cache, download, and read JSON release assets.
Changed controller wrapper installation to prefer GitHub release binary artifacts and only build internally when download/target resolution fails.

releases.title: GitHub-first binary compilation and component-owned versions
releases.summary: Split release binary compilation into a dedicated GitHub-first stage and stop treating PAC controller releases as component version bumps.
changes: Added scripts/compile-release-binaries.py to compile every Go binary component before release packaging.
Updated the PAC release workflow to compile binaries first and publish a separate pac-binaries.zip asset.
Changed release package generation to consume the precompiled release-binaries manifest instead of building one endpoint binary internally.
Updated package manifest generation with component-owned version policy metadata so unchanged components are not tied to the PAC controller version.
Changed source update detection to prefer component content hashes, so unchanged sources remain current and changed sources still surface even if a component version was not bumped.

releases.title: Focused setup wizard blockers
releases.summary: Make the reload setup wizard explain only the remaining required configuration instead of presenting every possible setup option.
changes: Added a setup wizard planning module that maps required setup issues to focused wizard steps.
The reload wizard now opens on a Remaining overview that summarizes active blockers and their required steps.
Wizard navigation is filtered to relevant steps, with issue count badges for affected steps.
The first-model flow includes the provider step only when it is needed as a prerequisite.
The connection step can now fix the default dev token without forcing a controller public URL update.

releases.title: Robust environment debug bundle digests
changes: Fixed environment debug bundle generation for Session models that do not have provider, endpoint, or profile attributes.
Made session, task, active-task, and event digest generation per-item safe so one incompatible record cannot collapse the whole bundle into the fallback zip.
Session diagnostics now derive provider, endpoint, and active model fields from session metadata when the direct model fields are not present.
Generation errors are recorded in generation/errors.json while still producing the normal support bundle contents.

releases.title: Reliable environment debug bundle generation
releases.summary: Harden the global Downloads debug bundle workflow so generation produces a downloadable artifact even when one diagnostic collector fails.
changes: Made environment debug bundle collection section-safe so sessions, tasks, events, logs, latest-session diagnostics, and platform snapshots can fail independently without aborting the whole bundle.
Added generation/errors.json and support-summary warnings so bundle-generation problems are visible inside the downloadable zip.
Added a top-level fallback zip if an unexpected collector error escapes the normal builder, preventing the global Downloads action from returning only a 500 where possible.
Changed the Downloads modal to render download buttons only after a bundle exists and to fetch bundle files with PAC authorization headers.
Exposed tokenHeaders for shared authenticated downloads and improved the empty state copy to make the generate-first workflow clear.

releases.title: Global environment debug bundle downloads
changes: Added generated environment debug bundles to the global Downloads modal instead of only the per-session thought inspector.
Added API routes to list, generate, download, and delete environment debug bundles under /v1/debug-bundles.
Debug bundles are generated first into the PAC data directory, then exposed as downloadable artifacts once ready.
Environment bundles include redacted sessions, tasks, active tasks, recent events, runtime snapshots, process/port/container/service state, and recent PAC log tails.
Added a Generate debug bundle action and refreshable bundle list in the Downloads area so support data can be collected without pasting shell commands.

releases.title: Session debug bundle download
releases.summary: Add a thought-inspector debug bundle download that collects the session, platform snapshots, and recent logs needed for troubleshooting stuck or confusing PAC behavior.
changes: Added a session-scoped /v1/sessions/{session_id}/debug-bundle.zip endpoint that builds a redacted platform support archive.
Added a Generate and download debug button next to Close in the thought/details modal.
Included session events, tasks, active task metadata, config, workspace state, process/port/container/service snapshots, and recent PAC log tails in the debug zip.
Added redaction for obvious tokens, API keys, bearer values, cookies, passwords, and secrets before writing support files.
Documented the debug bundle contents and follow-up scope improvements.

releases.title: Readable thought inspector details
releases.summary: Make the session thought/details modal useful by showing collapsed runtime phases, clear elapsed timing, a human explanation, and inspectable event details.
changes: Collapsed duplicate runtime phase events into readable phase rows with Step N labels, relative offsets, and explicit duration/status text.
Added a What happened section that summarizes the current active phase, recent tool activity, and attention/error signals.
Added an inspectable event log with expandable event bodies and raw event data so users can see what happened without hunting through full logs.
Added a run window with start time, last event time, recorded span, and internal event count.
Documented the thought inspector state in docs/PAC_THOUGHT_INSPECTOR_STATUS.md.

releases.title: Provider call stop and stream watchdogs
releases.summary: Make running agent tasks stoppable while a provider stream or blocking model call is in progress, and add default decision/stream wall-clock timeouts.
changes: Added abort-aware polling to the blocking provider-call helper so stop/cancel requests are noticed while the provider thread is still running.
Added a model_call_cancelled event when PAC abandons an in-flight provider call due to user stop or cancellation.
Added a default 180 second decision model timeout, overridable through task or session metadata.
Added wall-clock budgets for OpenAI-compatible streaming calls so active token or reasoning streams cannot run indefinitely.
Updated agent-loop handling so a stop during decision-model execution completes cleanly instead of waiting for the provider stream to end.
Documented provider call control status and runtime timeout knobs.

releases.title: Mid-session model hot-swap and fallback chains
releases.summary: Add /model session switching with provider-qualified selectors, capability checks, model history, fallback chains, and sub-agent role preference integration.
changes: Added a model_switch service to resolve exact, alias, and provider-qualified selectors such as lmstudio:<model-id>.
Added /model slash command support to list available models or switch the active session model.
Added model capability and provider availability checks before committing a switch, with warnings for weak structured-agent support.
Stored model_history and model_fallback_chain in session metadata and surfaced model_switched/model_switch_failed timeline events.
Updated slash_command tool handling so agents can request /model switches from inside an agent loop.
Updated model fallback resolution so active session fallback chains are preferred after empty/failing model responses.
Extended sub-agent model selection to honor per-role model preferences for Explore, Plan, Coder, Verify, and General-purpose work.
Added model hot-swap status documentation and slash-command UI help.

releases.title: Doom-loop detection and forced recovery
releases.summary: Add repeated tool-sequence detection so PAC can stop guessed-path and repeated-error loops and force a discovery-based recovery path.
changes: Added agent_doom_loop with compact tool/outcome fingerprints stored per task.
Detected repeated missing-path reads, repeated failing tool outcomes, and repeated short tool sequences.
Added doom_loop_detected events with timeline-card metadata showing the matched pattern and selected recovery tool.
The agent loop now forces read-only recovery through workspace_manifest, find_code_paths, or list_files instead of letting the same failed action continue.
If a model continues the same failing tool family after recovery has started, PAC overrides it with another discovery step before execution.
Updated session prompt guidance and timeline event listeners for doom-loop recovery behavior.
Added documentation for current detector scope and remaining follow-up work.

releases.title: LM Studio local inference discovery and registration
releases.summary: Add PAC-aligned local inference support by discovering external LM Studio servers, health-checking them, and registering them as local providers with model inventory.
changes: Added LocalInferenceDiscoveryService-style LM Studio discovery for localhost, host.docker.internal, endpoint-derived hosts, and manually supplied URLs.
Added LM Studio health checks for OpenAI-compatible model inventory and optional tiny chat-completion validation.
Added /v1/local-inference/lmstudio/discover, /health, and /register API routes.
Added local_inference_discover, local_inference_health, and local_inference_register agent tools routed through the ToolPipeline.
Added one-click LM Studio discovery and registration from the Providers page.
Registration now stores LM Studio as a local provider, caches discovered models, and can create PAC model entries from the live inventory.
Added local inference status documentation and updated controller prompt/tool guidance.

releases.title: Playbook builder, cooperative cancellation, and Observe metrics
releases.summary: Finish the planned playbook additions with a visual builder, stronger cancellation propagation, and playbook observability metrics.
changes: Added a visual playbook builder modal that generates valid playbook YAML from guided fields and JSON parameter/step rows.
Added a Create playbook action to the Playbooks page alongside import/export and run controls.
Strengthened playbook cancellation by propagating stop_requested, cancel_requested, and stop_reason metadata to active child tasks.
Updated the agent loop to treat playbook cancellation metadata like user stop requests before and after model/tool work.
Added playbook metrics snapshots and /v1/observability/playbooks for Observe integration.
Added an Observe Playbooks card with run status counts, active runs, top run pressure, and average terminal duration.
Updated playbook documentation to reflect current behavior and remaining live-validation work.

releases.title: Playbook import/export, cancellation, and step targeting
releases.summary: Finish the next playbook workflow layer with YAML import/export, run cancellation, richer conditions, step output exports, and targeting later steps at sessions created earlier in the playbook.
changes: Added playbook YAML import/export APIs and agent tools, with UI controls for exporting catalog entries and importing custom YAML definitions.
Added playbook run cancellation, including child task cancellation markers and playbook_run_cancelled events.
Added richer playbook conditions for parameters, step outputs, status checks, contains, and all/any/not condition groups.
Added step output capture and export mapping so later steps can consume values produced by earlier tool or agent steps.
Updated the git-workspace-session built-in playbook so the inspect step targets the newly-created coding session instead of the original controller session.
Expanded the Playbooks UI with output details, created-session visibility, cancel controls, and a YAML import modal.

releases.title: First-class playbook workflows
changes: Added YAML playbook schema support with typed parameters, dependency ordering, conditions, checkpoints, and Confirm/Review/Approve gates.
Added persistent playbook run state under the PAC data directory with resume and approval operations.
Added built-in playbooks for git workspace coding sessions and code-change workflows.
Added Playbooks API routes for catalog, runs, resume, and approve operations.
Added playbook agent tools and ToolPipeline schemas/policies for playbook_list, playbook_start, playbook_status, playbook_resume, and playbook_approve.
Added a Playbooks UI page with catalog cards, editable JSON parameters, run progress, resume, and approval controls.
releases.summary: Add first-class YAML playbook workflows with typed parameters, gates, resume, API, tools, and UI.
Add readable LSP timeline cards and first C# Roslyn-readiness analysis.
changes: Added code_intelligence.timeline_cards to emit compact session timeline cards for LSP server status, document symbols, definitions, references, hover, call/type hierarchy, rename previews, endpoint LSP preparation, and C# analysis.

Added code_roslyn_analysis as a first C# semantic-readiness tool that discovers solutions/projects, extracts C# types, methods, inheritance/implementation relationships, checks dotnet availability, and optionally runs dotnet build --no-restore diagnostics.
Routed code_roslyn_analysis through endpoint/container execution for coding sessions so C# checks can run inside the selected endpoint image when available.
Registered code_roslyn_analysis in ToolPipeline typed schemas, read-only/path policy, the default PAC tool list, and specialist sub-agent locked tool sets.
Updated the code intelligence status document to reflect LSP cards, rename tooling, endpoint metadata, and remaining endpoint-daemon/Roslyn work.

releases.summary: Add persistent JSON-RPC language-server clients for exact code intelligence.
changes: Added persistent controller-side LSP client management with JSON-RPC stdio framing, initialization, didOpen, request routing, active-client tracking, and shutdown.
Added persistent LSP support for rust-analyzer, pyright-langserver or pylsp, gopls, typescript-language-server, and csharp-ls when those binaries are installed.
Added code_lsp_status, code_lsp_document_symbols, code_lsp_definition, code_lsp_references, code_lsp_hover, code_lsp_call_hierarchy, code_lsp_type_hierarchy, and code_lsp_shutdown tools.
Registered LSP tools in the ToolPipeline schema and path/read-only policy so they receive normal validation, permission checks, caching, and session tool gating.
Exposed the LSP tools to the main PAC agent and specialist sub-agent locked tool sets.
Updated the code intelligence status document to distinguish static analysis, endpoint diagnostics, and persistent controller-side LSP capabilities.

releases.summary: Add first-pass cross-language code intelligence tools and specialist access.
changes: Added code_intelligence_report to detect project markers, supported language mix, local LSP/tool availability, and a static symbol index.
Added code_symbol_search, code_definition, and code_references for Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, Rust, and C# source trees.
Added code_diagnostics to prepare or explicitly run safe diagnostics such as cargo check JSON, compileall, go test, tsc, and dotnet build when binaries are available.
Registered code intelligence tools in the ToolPipeline schema/policy layer so they get path sanity, file-read permission checks, caching, batching, and normal tool events.
Bridged code_diagnostics through endpoint runner execution for container-backed coding sessions so cargo/go/tsc/dotnet checks can run inside the session image.
Added the code-intelligence tools to main and specialist sub-agent tool sets so Explore, Plan, Coder, Verify, and General-purpose agents can use symbol-aware inspection.
Documented current code-intelligence coverage and the remaining work for full LSP/Roslyn/rust-analyzer integration.

releases.summary: Sync endpoint-advertised tool schemas into the ToolPipeline and expose schema errors.
changes: Added endpoint heartbeat tool schema extraction for capabilities.tool_schemas, capabilities.tools, metadata.tool_schemas, and legacy agent_tools payloads.

Added endpoint-to-ToolConfig sync so endpoint-advertised tools can publish argument schemas, permission classes, read-only/mutating behavior, path fields, and cache policy without hand-editing controller config.
Endpoint heartbeat now persists discovered tool schemas and emits endpoint_tool_schemas_synced or endpoint_tool_schemas_seen events.
Added ToolPipeline schema summaries and tool_pipeline_schema_invalid events with expected fields for better UI rendering and troubleshooting.
Updated the Tool Pipeline status document to mark endpoint discovery/sync as implemented and clarify remaining UI, cache, hooks, parallelism, and metrics work.

releases.summary: Add dynamic tool schemas and granular pipeline permission classes.
changes: Extended ToolConfig with argument_schema, permission_class, mutating/read_only, path_scoped, path_fields, and cache_policy metadata for endpoint and package tools.
Extended PluginConfig so plugins can publish tool argument schemas that are merged into the same ToolPipeline schema registry as built-in tools.
Added dynamic schema translation for JSON-Schema-like and PAC-native tool schema definitions, including required fields, properties, enums, ranges, array item types, any-of, and one-of constraints.
Updated ToolPipeline schema validation, path scoping, cache decisions, and mutating/read-only policy to use dynamic tool metadata when present.
Added explicit pipeline permission classes for git_write and pac_control_plane_write, and routed PAC control-plane creation tools and auto_commit through those classes.
Documented the new dynamic schema and policy coverage in the pipeline status document.

releases.summary: Strengthen ToolPipeline schema validation and document remaining pipeline finalization work.
changes: Added pipeline_schema with typed built-in tool specs for required fields, primitive argument types, ranges, enums, array item validation, and any-of field groups.
ToolPipeline now validates tool inputs against typed specs before path checks, approval policy, runner dispatch, or handler execution.
Moved required-field lookup onto the typed schema registry while keeping pipeline_policy focused on tool categories, path scoping, capability aliases, and plan-mode policy.
Added schema coverage for workspace, file, code locator, web, shell, background shell, git, artifact, memory, model-consult, PAC control-plane, task-control, and PTY tools.
Added docs/PAC_AGENT_TOOL_PIPELINE_STATUS.md to record current pipeline coverage and the remaining work needed before this area is complete.

releases.summary: Move approval and command policy decisions into the shared agent ToolPipeline.
changes: Added pipeline_approval as the shared approval/auto-approval helper for agent tool calls.
ToolPipeline now runs a dedicated approval stage after capability checks and before execution/cache lookup, covering shell command policy, file-write approvals, and network approvals.
Pipeline approval now records pending_tool metadata and approval_required events consistently instead of leaving each handler to format its own pause state.
Shell command policy is evaluated by the pipeline before local or endpoint-runner execution so container-backed shell tools follow the same approval contract as local tools.
write_file, web_search, web_fetch, and shell handlers now honor pipeline-approved calls and avoid duplicate approval prompts.
Added tool_pipeline_denied events for policy denials emitted by the pipeline.

releases.summary: Route every agent tool call through a shared ToolPipeline guardrail service.
changes: Added ToolPipeline as the shared outer execution contract for agent tools, covering argument parsing, schema checks, workspace path sanity, plan-mode guardrails, capability checks, cache hooks, artifact storage, and invalidation.
Moved tool pipeline policy into a dedicated pipeline_policy module so tool categories, required fields, path-scoped tools, and session capability aliases are explicit and reusable.
Updated the agent tool registry so runner-backed tools and all local handler-dispatched tools pass through the same pipeline before execution.
Added pipeline timeline events for pipeline start, cache hits, large-result artifact storage, completion, and cache invalidation.
Updated controller prompt guidance to tell the model that tool inputs must use valid JSON and workspace-relative paths because the pipeline enforces those constraints.

releases.summary: Add a coding-session readiness contract before agent execution.
changes: Added CodingSessionReadinessService to make coding sessions prove endpoint, workspace, source checkout, and agent attachment readiness before the loop runs.

Git-backed coding sessions now prepare both the controller workspace and the selected endpoint workspace, cloning the repository on the endpoint when needed so container tools see the same source tree.

Session creation and PAC controller-created sessions now record coding_readiness metadata and emit coding_session_readiness events for endpoint selection, workspace preparation, and ready/failed states.
The agent loop now blocks and fails clearly when a coding session is not ready instead of letting the model inspect an empty or disconnected workspace.

releases.summary: Connect PAC-created git programming sessions to a container-capable pi.dev endpoint.
changes: PAC resource-created programming sessions now carry coding_session, agent_enabled, container execution, container image, preferred endpoint, and endpoint lock metadata instead of only creating a passive session record.
The git workspace/session intent payload now passes container_image metadata to pac_create_session so prompts asking for a Rust container create a Rust-backed programming session.
pac_create_session resolves an online container-capable endpoint when a workspace profile or session request requires a container runtime, and fails clearly if none is available.
session_created events for PAC-created sessions now include endpoint, execution mode, container image, and agent enabled details to make future stuck-session diagnosis visible in the timeline.

releases.summary: Run PAC workspace/session resource creation as a deterministic control-plane flow.
changes: Added a deterministic PAC resource flow so git workspace requests execute workspace profile creation and programming session creation back-to-back without waiting for another model decision turn.
PAC resource requests now complete with an evidence summary after the requested profile/session are created, preventing the agent from drifting into PAC/core workspace inspection.
Workspace profile creation is now idempotent for controller-generated resource requests, so retrying the same git workspace prompt does not fail just because the profile already exists.
Resource flow actions pass idempotent metadata and preserve workspace materialization details in the final session summary.
Made PAC resource-intent detection robust for git workspace prompts that include repository URLs even when the imperative text is imperfect or split.
Git-backed workspace creation now implicitly requires a programming session unless the user explicitly asks for a workspace profile only.
Prevented git workspace requests from falling back into PAC/core codebase inspection when the user expects a new session with cloned source.

releases.summary: Materialize git workspace profiles when PAC controller tools create programming sessions.
changes: Added a shared workspace bootstrap helper that creates local workspace directories and safely clones git-backed workspaces.
PAC-created sessions now materialize the requested workspace before saving the session, so repository-backed sessions start with source files present.
The public session creation route now uses the same bootstrap helper instead of carrying separate inline git clone logic.
Tool execution now calls the shared workspace bootstrap so existing git/profile sessions with a URL can self-heal before file or shell tools run.
Session created events now include workspace_materialization details for troubleshooting whether a workspace was created, already cloned, or freshly cloned.

releases.summary: Expand PAC host diagnostics so troubleshooting bundles include controller logs and service state.
changes: Updated scripts/collect-pac-host-debug.sh to detect the PAC install root when run from /home/dorbian/.pacp/app and collect sibling logs from /home/dorbian/.pacp/logs.
Added user and system pacp.service status plus focused pacp journals to the diagnostics bundle so restart loops and service failures are visible.
Added PAC container logs, recent state indexes, and broader pi-agent artifact discovery while preserving secret redaction.
Increased default log tails to 600 lines and kept the collector as a single short command for future support exchanges.

releases.summary: Add safer changed-files update handling and readable long session prompts.
changes: Changed-files PAC update zips are now detected as merge patches instead of full app packages, avoiding accidental replacement of whole directories from a partial archive.
The app updater now accepts patch zips that contain VERSION, PAC_CHANGELOG.json, and PAC_UPDATE_DIFF*.diff even when they only touch non-Python files such as GitHub workflows.
Update previews show whether an upload is a changed-files patch or a full app package before applying it.
Long session prompts and large assistant/tool text now collapse by default with show, copy, and download controls so pasted diagnostics remain readable.
Added scripts/collect-pac-host-debug.sh as a small reusable host diagnostics collector instead of sending a wall of Bash in chat.

releases.summary: Fix release workflow YAML indentation for the embedded Python version update script.
changes: Indented the embedded python3 heredoc body and closing delimiter inside the pac-release.yml run block so GitHub Actions can parse the workflow as valid YAML.
Kept the shell heredoc delimiter valid after YAML block-scalar indentation stripping so the Python snippet still executes as intended.
Updated release metadata for the corrected workflow patch.

releases.summary: Make the GitHub release workflow token handling explicit and fail-fast for package publishing.
changes: Updated the release workflow to reference the PUSH_TOKEN secret explicitly for cross-repository package publishing.
Kept the controller checkout able to fall back to github.token while using the conventional uppercase secret name.
Added a fail-fast packages token preflight before checking out pac-labs/packages so missing credentials produce a clear error.
Normalized the patched release workflow to LF line endings.

releases.summary: Route PAC workspace/session creation prompts through control-plane tools instead of unsafe file reads.
changes: Added PAC resource intent parsing for requests that create git workspaces and programming sessions.
Request-intent bootstrap now uses pac_create_workspace_profile for repository workspace creation instead of treating repository URLs as local file paths.
Unsafe read_file bootstraps for http(s), git, domain, absolute, or parent-relative paths are replaced with a safer intent fallback.
Added a PAC resource final-answer contract that forces the requested workspace profile and session creation steps before finalizing.
Session creation generated from a workspace/session prompt now targets the created workspace profile and exposes programming-capable platform tools.

releases.summary: Enforce a stronger implementation-loop contract for PAC code-change work.
changes: Added agent_work_contract.py to keep code-change grounding separate from the main agent loop.
PAC code-change requests now require workspace_manifest before other code work when the request is not already grounded by contract state.
After the manifest, the loop forces find_code_paths/ripgrep/fd before opening or editing files.
Mutating tool calls are blocked until at least one verified source file has been read.
Final answers are converted into git_diff until a validation/inspection step occurs after mutation.
Updated controller prompt guidance to describe the enforced implementation contract.

releases.summary: Treat imperative PAC/code changes as executable work instead of answer-only chat.
changes: Classified prompts such as add, persist, store, enable, and allow as PAC work requests when they imply code or runtime changes.
Request-intent fallback now starts those code-change requests with workspace_manifest when no exact file path is named.
Final-answer policy now rejects premature summaries for code-change requests until the transcript includes a mutating tool step such as write_file or a scoped shell edit.
Controller guidance now states that explicit requests to look online for documentation should use web_search/web_fetch during implementation planning.

releases.summary: Wire the new PAC/core self-creation tools into the built-in controller context and session.
changes: PAC/core now carries the new controller tooling set, including find_code_paths and the pac_create_* control-plane helpers.
The main pi.dev profile now backfills its baseline tool list on load so newly added controller tools become available to the built-in system context.
Existing controller sessions now sync to the updated PAC/core tool set instead of rejecting the new self-creation tools as unavailable.

releases.summary: Add PAC/core code-location and self-creation tools for the built-in controller session.
changes: Added find_code_paths so PAC/core can locate likely implementation files for concepts like session timeline, dashboard atlas, providers, and endpoints before opening files.
Added PAC control-plane tools for the built-in controller session to list and create providers, models, endpoints, workspace profiles, and sessions without editing YAML by hand.
Improved controller-session prompting and request-intent routing so PAC prefers verified paths and PAC component tooling for broad codebase and self-configuration requests.

releases.summary: Stop runaway missing-file chunk loops and keep session thinking timers anchored to the start of the task.
changes: Agent loop now detects repeated read_file_chunk misses and tells the model to re-locate the file instead of blindly requesting higher chunk indexes forever.
Chunk readers now stop escalating when the target file is missing or the chunk index is out of range, which prevents runaway controller-session loops.
Composer thinking duration now derives from the earliest task event instead of being reset by newer internal events during polling.

releases.summary: Keep PAC/core on the selected executor model while routing controller-session decisions directly to a structured model when needed.
changes: PAC/core and other controller-harness sessions now route immediately to a structured decision model when the selected executor model is not suitable for agent-loop work.
OpenAI-compatible and LM Studio streaming now detect reasoning-only streams and retry the same model without streaming before PAC treats the turn as empty.
This keeps a controller session visibly configured on models like DECK while avoiding the wasted first empty turn before PAC falls back to a tool/JSON-capable model.

releases.summary: Make PAC/core model selection persist across restart and keep controller-session model metadata aligned with the built-in agent context.
changes: Stopped startup scaffolding from overwriting PAC/core executor_model with the controller harness default on every boot.
Made the controller harness session derive model, profile, permissions, and model-role metadata from the PAC/core context when that system context exists.
Cleared stale executor/planner/reviewer/retrieval metadata from the controller session when the context no longer defines them.

releases.summary: Recover empty model turns by failing over to another configured model and make model tests reflect realistic LM Studio output budgets.
changes: When a selected model returns empty output repeatedly, PAC now retries once with another configured model instead of looping empty and failing immediately.
Model test calls for OpenAI-compatible and LM Studio providers now use a more realistic output budget and deterministic temperature so the UI better reflects live behavior.
This specifically improves controller-session recovery when a context is intentionally set to a model like DECK that can return blank completions under the full PAC prompt context.

releases.summary: Fixed the 1.0.367 agent-routing startup regression and preserved the structured-model fallback for agent work.
changes: Fixed the agent model selection module import so PAC starts correctly after the structured agent-routing refactor.
Kept the structured decision-model fallback so context-bound work sessions can use a tool and JSON-capable model for agent-loop work when the selected executor model is not suitable.
Kept the OpenAI-compatible retry path for reasoning-only empty completions that stop with a too-small token budget.

releases.summary: Improved agent model routing so context-bound work sessions recover from reasoning-only empty completions and use a structured decision model when the executor is not suitable for agent work.
changes: Added agent-model selection for work requests so PAC can keep the chosen executor model for simple chat while switching structured agent decisions onto a tool/JSON-capable model when the selected executor is not suitable for agent-loop work.

Recorded explicit model-routing issue events and effective decision-model metadata so PAC shows when a context-bound session is using a different decision model for agent work than the visible executor model.
Improved OpenAI-compatible provider handling for reasoning-only empty completions by retrying with a larger token budget before PAC treats the response as empty.

releases.summary: Fixed chat regressions that leaked internal agent-policy and context-sync events into the visible session timeline.
changes: Classified final-answer policy events, action-narration recovery events, and agent-context session sync events as internal timeline activity so they stay in the thinking flow instead of rendering as user-facing replies.
Marked backend agent-context session sync events as internal/system events at the source so the chat UI can reliably hide them across refreshes and snapshot reloads.
Filtered short internal recovery/status messages such as 'Prepared a final answer' and safe bootstrap narration from assistant reply rendering to keep PAC controller sessions focused on real answers.

releases.summary: Synced context-bound sessions to the latest agent context model before routing new work.
changes: Added a context-bound session resync step to the session task creation path so existing sessions re-apply the latest agent context runtime fields before PAC routes a new prompt.
Fixed the stale-model case where updating an agent context executor model appeared to save but the backing session continued to run the older concrete session model.
Verified live on PAC/core that changing the executor model and then sending a prompt updates the existing PAC controller pi.dev session model immediately.

releases.summary: Made controller work requests bootstrap into concrete inspection work before the main agent turn instead of stalling in narration or unconditional planning.
changes: Added request-intent resolution for agent-loop work requests, with a hidden runtime setting for the intent model and a deterministic heuristic fallback when the intent model is unavailable or returns unusable output.

Changed broad PAC/core work requests so the loop resolves the first bootstrap tool before prompt expansion, skips the automatic workspace index when a bootstrap inspection step is already chosen, and avoids planning for read-only overview requests like workspace indexing and codebase explanation.

Tightened final-answer policy for work requests and compacted workspace manifest tool results before refeeding them to the model so controller sessions progress through actual inspection work instead of emitting early summaries or oversized context dumps.

releases.summary: Forwarded controller pi.dev session traffic into a configurable agent inbox so runtime-side agents can observe PAC messages and results directly.
changes: Added a configurable controller-harness event forwarder that mirrors selected session events such as user messages, task queueing, intents, tool activity, failures, and final results into pi-agent-artifacts/agent-forwarding.jsonl.
Made the forwarding scope configurable so PAC can stay focused on the built-in PAC/core controller session now while remaining extensible for future pi.dev-backed or additional agent runtimes.
Surfaced the forwarding artifact in pi.dev observability and post-update diagnostics so update verification can confirm that the runtime inbox path is present.

releases.summary: Stabilized the local pi.dev container by rebuilding it deterministically from source and removing the broken npm CLI fallback.
changes: Updated the pi.dev harness image build so it includes pacctl, records a source version label, and can be rebuilt consistently from the PAC source tree.
Removed the implicit npx fallback from the harness runtime unless it is explicitly enabled, which stops the repeated 'could not determine executable to run' npm failure in daemon mode.
Made PAC rebuild and restart the local pi.dev image during release updates and when the installed harness image no longer matches the current source version.

releases.summary: Integrated the latest redesign graphics pass while preserving pi.dev monitoring and release verification details.
changes: Merged the latest redesign shell and dashboard presentation pass, including the refined overview layout, shell spacing, and new session action styling modules.
Split the PAC Component Atlas into its own dedicated page so the dashboard overview can focus on health, activity, readiness, and utilization cards.
Preserved pi.dev-specific runtime surfaces so the update result, runtime settings page, controller harness panel, and Observe page still expose wrapper, pi-agent, and pacctl health details.

releases.summary: Surfaced real pi.dev runtime logs and added post-update pi.dev health verification.
changes: Exposed pi-agent.log and pacctl.log through controller diagnostics and the Observe/Runtime UI so the real pi.dev runtime path is visible without entering the container.
Added post-update verification that checks the controller pi.dev session, wrapper process, daemon state, and recent runtime log errors after every PAC release install.
Extended the Update Center result details so release applies report whether pi.dev is healthy immediately after the update.

releases.summary: Extended the redesign with a more capable atlas overview, restart visibility, and update interaction polish.
changes: Integrated the latest redesign pass for atlas layout and interaction behavior, including broader dashboard health surfacing and topology refinements.
Updated the release confirmation and restart visibility experience so release apply flow better matches the redesigned shell.
Added the new redesign styling modules for brand readability, dashboard overview presentation, and release apply restart visibility.

releases.summary: Extended the redesign with richer atlas interaction layout and an operational events retention surface.
changes: Improved atlas layout for provider, model, and capability clusters so the dashboard map stays readable as those groups grow.
Refined atlas pan behavior to prevent accidental text selection while dragging the canvas.
Added the next Events hub redesign pass, including retention status, editable retention policy, and manual event pruning controls.
Implemented backend support for urgent event feeds, event summaries, and event retention policy management so the redesigned Events hub works end to end.

releases.summary: Extended the redesigned UI shell with a standardized page runtime, global utilities, and an Update Center surface.
changes: Added the next shell-runtime layer from the redesign update, including shared page helpers, update-center runtime wiring, and the standardized app-shell styling.
Refined the redesign navigation and account/session shell behavior while keeping session selection and update visibility working against the live host.
Verified the updated shell on 0.7 by selecting a session and opening the Update Center successfully.

releases.summary: Integrated the redesigned PAC UI shell, navigation, sessions, configuration, and source workbench surfaces.
changes: Replaced the legacy mixed navigation with the redesigned shell navigation and grouped control-plane layout.
Integrated the redesigned session, source workbench, admin configuration, provider, profile, and endpoint frontend modules.
Split the frontend into the new modular redesign structure and verified core navigation, session selection, and update visibility against the live 0.7 host.

releases.summary: Fixed the compact navigation boot regression that broke session and admin interactions.
changes: Exported the compact navigation renderer through the shared tab-group state so the overflow logic no longer crashes on page load.
Verified session selection and the PAC update check flow end to end in a browser session against the live 0.7 host.

releases.summary: Improved the PAC Component Atlas with zoom, pan, and collision-aware layout.
changes: Added wheel-zoom and drag-pan interactions for the dashboard atlas viewport.
Split atlas-specific interaction and layout logic into dedicated dashboard modules to keep the topology renderer focused.
Changed atlas layout to size groups dynamically and avoid overlapping lanes as the graph grows.
Updated atlas edge routing to use the actual laid-out node dimensions rather than fixed centers.

releases.summary: Validated the release-bundled controller wrapper update path on a live PAC host.
changes: Published a follow-up release from the same bundled-binary updater code so PAC could verify the release install path on a runtime already containing the new updater logic.
Confirmed the release package structure continues to include the prebuilt pac-endpoint wrapper binaries used by controller updates.

releases.summary: Bundled controller wrapper binaries into GitHub releases and taught the updater to install them before restart.
changes: Generate pac-full.zip with prebuilt pac-endpoint binaries for Linux, macOS, and Windows targets used by controller and endpoint update flows.
Updated the GitHub release workflow to stamp pyproject.toml with the release version and provision Go before packaging bundled binaries.
Changed the PAC updater to install the bundled host wrapper binary from the release archive before falling back to a local rebuild.
Kept local rebuild behavior as a fallback path for development or incomplete release archives instead of the default release-update mechanism.

releases.summary: PAC v1.0.351 release generated by GitHub Actions.
changes: PAC release artifact generated by GitHub Actions.

releases.summary: PAC v1.0.350 release generated by GitHub Actions.
changes: PAC release artifact generated by GitHub Actions.

releases.summary: Made the agent loop lighter by classifying requests, caching workspace indexes, and planning only when needed.
changes: Added request classification so greetings, simple direct actions, and local fact questions can skip heavyweight planning and workspace indexing.
Cached workspace indexes per session with a short TTL and invalidation on file writes so repeated coding turns stop rebuilding the same project snapshot.
Changed session task creation so planning is no longer forced on every turn; explicit /plan still works, while normal turns rely on request complexity.
Reduced controller-session overhead by only emitting fresh workspace indexing when PAC actually rebuilds the index instead of on every request.

releases.summary: Refine session thought disclosure into a ChatGPT-style conversation line and fix thought details modal closing.
changes: Changed session thought rows to show a compact "Thinking for" / "Thought for" disclosure line instead of the current intent as the primary message.
Kept current intent/status visible only while active as subtle secondary text, with full timing/details available through the details modal.
Stopped final-response intent text from surfacing as "Preparing final response" in the conversation stream.
Bound the thought/details modal close button, backdrop click, and Escape key from the sessions UI so details can be reliably closed.
Kept raw model/tool output out of the conversation thought row; full execution detail remains in the full session log and details modal.

releases.summary: Rendered assistant replies and thought/intent status as clean conversation content without redundant labels or timestamps.
changes: Changed assistant replies to render as open conversation content instead of boxed agent bubbles.
Removed visible Agent labels and timestamps from assistant replies while keeping metadata available through reply details.
Changed thought/intent history rows into compact status lines without visible Thought for or Thinking for time text.
Kept thought timing, status, tools, and event counts available in the detail modal instead of the main conversation stream.
Preserved user message bubbles and approval/system warning bubbles so ownership and required actions remain clear.

releases.summary: Moved session thinking status into the conversation history as an intent/timing bubble and kept detailed execution output in the full log.
changes: Changed the session thought timer to start from PAC task/model processing events instead of user message rendering.
Changed thought bubbles to display the current agent intent as the primary conversation-history item until the task completes.
Removed response/tool step expansion from the composer thinking strip so detailed model responses and tool output remain in the full session log.
Stopped creating pending thought rows directly from user messages; PAC now waits for processing/runtime events before showing active thinking.
Adjusted thought detail modals to summarize timing and point users to the full log for raw model/tool diagnostics.

releases.summary: Hardened GitHub release generation against malformed legacy changelog JSON and normalized release metadata handling.
changes: Made generate-pac-release.py tolerate safely repairable trailing commas in PAC_CHANGELOG.json and fail with clearer line/column diagnostics for unrecoverable JSON errors.
Normalized release generation to the existing top-level releases changelog format instead of introducing a parallel entries list.
Preserved legacy entries by folding them into releases during release generation so existing changelog history is not lost.
Ensured generated release entries include date, summary, and changes fields matching the PAC changelog shape.

releases.summary: Refined the session composer into a conversational PAC composer with context pills, attachment chips, and clearer send/runtime controls.
changes: Replaced the command-bar style session composer with a rounded conversational panel modeled around message entry, context state, runtime controls, and a clear send button.
Added live composer pills for mode, agent context, selected model, and endpoint so users can see what PAC will use before sending.
Added an attachment/context tray with removable chips for files, images, runtime choices, and inserted context instructions.
Moved slash help, add-context, runtime destination, permission quick controls, and send actions into a compact composer action row.
Updated composer autosizing and status text so the input feels more like a chat composer while preserving existing slash commands and session routing.

releases.summary: Separated PAC release automation into manual main releases, approved PR releases, and safe diff PR expansion.
changes: Replaced conflicting diff-release workflows with a diff expansion workflow that turns .pac/diffs input files into normal source changes before review.
Added an approved pull request release workflow that verifies PR readiness, merges approved PRs into main, and dispatches the main release workflow.
Updated the main release workflow so GitHub owns version resolution, release metadata commits, tag creation, release assets, and package sync.
Added diff validation and application helpers that support both git-style patches and diff -ruN pac_orig/pac_work patches while stripping release-owned metadata.
Documented the three supported release paths and clarified that VERSION, changelog, manifest, and pyproject versioning are owned by GitHub release automation.

releases.summary: Refactored profiles into behavior/context/access presets with card-based management and server-side group restrictions.
changes: Added a dedicated profiles API router and a card-based Profiles page with a top-level create action.
Refactored profiles so they define instructions, context policy, permission defaults, and group access instead of owning concrete models or tool lists.
Added server-side group checks for listing, reading, and using restricted profiles, including workspace-default profile authorization errors.
Stopped session creation from resolving concrete models or runtime tool lists from profile-owned model/tool fields.
Preserved legacy profile fields for compatibility while removing them from the active profile editor and runtime ownership model.

releases.summary: Split the monolithic web stylesheet into ordered CSS modules while preserving the existing cascade through a compatibility loader.
changes: Replaced pi_agent_platform/web/styles.css with a small import-only compatibility stylesheet.

Added ordered modules under pi_agent_platform/web/styles/ for foundation, dashboard, endpoints, sources, sessions, control-plane, source workbench, providers/admin, header/theme, light surfaces, release overlay, and mobile rules.
Kept the original cascade order by importing modules in the same source-range order as the former monolithic stylesheet.
Updated refactor and migration documentation with stylesheet ownership rules and the v1.0.284 CSS split checkpoint.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/styles.css from 5,119 lines to 19 lines; the largest new stylesheet module is 646 lines.

releases.summary: Completed the current safe web/app.js cleanup wave by extracting remaining frontend domains into classic-script modules.
changes: Added web/app/events.js for global event rail, event normalization, event tones, and update confirmation overlay helpers.
Added web/app/workspaces_contexts.js for shared storage, IDE context, agent context, user workspace, and workspace template UI helpers.
Added web/app/profiles_config.js for profile/tool/provider/model configuration forms, model sync helpers, and runtime form helpers.
Added web/app/admin_updates.js for proxy route, PAC update/archive/diff/release, package upload, restart, MCP bridge, and admin update helpers.
Added web/app/marketplace.js, web/app/personal_settings.js, and web/app/composer_status.js for remaining marketplace, personal settings, and composer status helpers.
Updated web/index.html to load the new modules before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 3,596 lines to 969 lines while preserving the current classic-script/no-bundler runtime model.

releases.summary: Split endpoint/dashboard/controller-runtime and auth/admin frontend helpers out of the monolithic web app script.
changes: Added web/app/endpoints.js for endpoint inventory, runner dashboard, endpoint command modal, service/TLS, controller harness, and endpoint connection UI helpers.
Added web/app/auth_admin.js for login/auth state, current-user chip, personal token helpers, user list rendering, and group/admin access UI helpers.
Updated web/index.html to load endpoints.js and auth_admin.js before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 4,951 lines to 3,596 lines while preserving the classic no-bundler script model.

releases.summary: Split session timeline, approval, composer, and session wizard frontend helpers out of the monolithic web app script.
changes: Added web/app/sessions.js for session timeline rendering, thinking/approval rows, assistant reply actions, session polling, composer/task helpers, session wizard helpers, container destination selection, prompt context insertion, and session git diff modal behavior.
Updated web/index.html to load sessions.js before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Kept the classic-script global function model to preserve current browser runtime behavior without introducing a bundler.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 6,721 lines to 4,951 lines.

releases.summary: Split source-library and source-coding frontend helpers out of the monolithic web app.js.
changes: Added web/app/sources.js for source tree, file tab, source build, binary artifact, feature-pack, marketplace, source context, source secret/variable, PAC RAM, and source coding UI helpers.
Updated web/index.html to load sources.js before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Kept the classic-script global function model to preserve current browser runtime behavior without introducing a bundler.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 8,172 lines to 6,721 lines.

releases.summary: Split provider and model catalog UI helpers out of the monolithic web app script.
changes: Added web/app/providers.js for provider/model availability helpers, provider health rendering, LM Studio actions, model draft helpers, recommendations, and live provider/model panels.
Updated web/index.html to load providers.js after provider presets and before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 8,829 lines to 8,172 lines while preserving the classic no-bundler script model.

releases.summary: Begin splitting the monolithic web app.js into behavior-preserving frontend helper modules.
changes: Added web/app/ui_helpers.js for shared HTML escaping helpers.
Added web/app/provider_presets.js for provider preset definitions and preset loading behavior.
Added web/app/session_commands.js for session slash-command parsing and help text.
Added web/app/setup_wizard.js for setup wizard rendering, step wiring, and save/complete behavior.
Updated web/index.html to load the helper scripts before the transitional app.js entrypoint.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/web/app.js from 9,303 lines to 8,829 lines.

releases.summary: Split proxy, server configuration, package upload, service runtime, TLS, and Let's Encrypt routes out of api/main.py.
changes: Added api/routes/proxy.py for proxy route management and reverse proxy forwarding endpoints.
Added api/routes/server_config.py for raw config updates and endpoint controller connection settings.
Added api/routes/package_upload.py for staged PAC package upload/apply routes.
Added api/routes/service_runtime.py for restart, service mode, TLS CA/certificate, and Let's Encrypt DNS-01 routes.
Removed duplicate root/app UI route declarations from api/main.py because static UI registration is already delegated to api/routes/ui.py.
Reduced pi_agent_platform/api/main.py from 3,923 lines to 3,527 lines.

releases.title: Specialist sub-agent chains and summary import
changes: Added run_subagent_chain for Explore → Plan → Coder → Verify specialist execution on larger code-change requests.
Added import_subagent_summary to bring completed child summaries back into the parent task context.
Added per-profile model preference resolution for Explore, Plan, Coder, Verify, and General-purpose sub-agents.
Added automatic specialist-chain selection for larger implementation/refactor prompts.
Added /chain slash command and parent-session sub-agent summary timeline panel.
Updated specialist sub-agent documentation with current behavior and remaining live-validation follow-ups.

releases.summary: Finalize the ToolPipeline pass with endpoint schema retirement, hooks, read-only batching, UI cards, and Observe metrics.
changes: Added per-stage ToolPipeline metrics and local trace spans for parse, schema, path, plan, capability, approval, cache, hook, execution, cache-write, and invalidation stages.
Added ToolPipeline hook extension points through ToolConfig pre_hooks and post_hooks with timeline events and guarded failure handling.
Added endpoint schema version/signature/last-seen/stale metadata and automatic retirement of endpoint-owned tools that disappear from heartbeat advertisements.
Added batch_tools for bounded parallel execution of read-only tool calls while rejecting mutating or nested calls.
Added structured timeline card payloads for tool_pipeline_schema_invalid so schema errors are visible without opening raw JSON.
Exposed tool pipeline metrics through metrics summary, observability metrics, and a dedicated /v1/observability/tool-pipeline endpoint.
Added an Observe page Tool pipeline card and updated the pipeline status document.

releases.title: Specialist sub-agent profiles
changes: Added locked Explore, Plan, Coder, Verify, and General-purpose sub-agent profiles with dedicated tool sets and turn budgets.
Added spawn_subagent as an agent tool and expanded slash commands for specialist sub-agents.
Sub-agent sessions now emit parent/child timeline events and completion summaries.
Agent loop now enforces max_agent_steps so specialist budgets stop runaway child sessions.
Documented current specialist sub-agent behavior and remaining follow-up work.

releases.title: Language-server code intelligence layer
changes: Added code_language_servers to report language-server and compiler availability for Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Go, Rust, and C#.
Added code_project_metadata for cargo metadata, go list, package.json summaries, Python project markers, and dotnet project discovery.
Added static code_call_hierarchy, code_type_hierarchy, code_module_index, and code_blast_radius tools for cross-file impact analysis.
Connected the new code intelligence tools to ToolPipeline schemas, path policies, the main PAC agent profile, and specialist sub-agent locked tool sets.
Allowed endpoint/container-backed coding sessions to run code_language_servers and code_project_metadata through the endpoint runner.
Updated code intelligence documentation with implemented capabilities and remaining full LSP/Roslyn work.

releases.title: LSP rename planning and endpoint preparation
changes: Added code_lsp_rename_plan for language-server rename workspace-edit previews with unified diffs.
Added code_lsp_rename_apply to apply LSP rename edits through file-write permission and pipeline approval.
Added code_lsp_endpoint_prepare so container-backed sessions can verify endpoint-side language server availability and write .pac/lsp metadata.
Routed code_lsp_status and endpoint LSP preparation through the endpoint runner for container coding sessions.
Registered the new LSP tools in ToolPipeline schemas, path policy, default tools, and specialist sub-agent tool sets.
Updated code intelligence documentation with the remaining endpoint JSON-RPC daemon and Roslyn follow-ups.
